blocks:
Ayahs

إِذَا ٱلسَّمَآءُ ٱنشَقَّتْ (۱)
When the sky is split open (1)
وَأَذِنَتْ لِرَبِّهَا وَحُقَّتْ (۲)
and gives ear to its Lord as it should. (2)
وَإِذَا ٱلْأَرْضُ مُدَّتْ (۳)
When the earth is spread out (3)
وَأَلْقَتْ مَا فِيهَا وَتَخَلَّتْ (٤)
and throws out what is in it, emptying itself, (4)
وَأَذِنَتْ لِرَبِّهَا وَحُقَّتْ (٥)
and gives ear to its Lord as it should. (5)

Key Points

| "Inshiqāq" means splitting open or cleaving. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «تنشق السماء من المجرة» (19), meaning the sky will be torn apart through the galaxy. "Adhinat" (أذِنت) is derived from "udhun" (اُذُن), meaning obedience or heeding, and "huqqat" (حُقَّتْ) means worthy, deserving, and that it was its right (or due). On the Day of Judgment, whatever is inside the earth—the dead, treasures, and so on—will be cast out. Just as He says in Sūrat Az-Zalzalah: «اذا زلزلت الارض زلزالها و اخرجت الارض اثقالها...»

19) Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr.

Messages

1- On the verge of the Day of Judgment, the firm and strong heavens «سبعاً شداداً» (20) will split open. «اذا السماء انشقت»

2- Existence is obedient and submissive to Allah. «اذنت لربّها» (This phrase is mentioned regarding both the heaven and the earth.)

3- Nature possesses a type of consciousness that makes it obedient to Allah. «اذنت لربّها»

4- Submission to God is the only submission that is worthy and deserving. «حُقّت»

5- On the verge of the Day of Judgment, the earth will be transformed such that all its unevenness disappears, becoming a vast and level surface. «اذا الارض مدّت» 20) An-Naba', 12.

Explanations

| Portrait of Sūrat Al-Inshiqāq. This Sūrah has twenty-five verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Sūrah is taken from the first verse and means "the splitting open." Like other Meccan Sūrahs, the central theme of this Sūrah is the subject of the Resurrection (Ma'ād) and the Day of Judgment (Qiyāmah). The disintegration of the existing cosmic order and the disruption of the order of the earth and the heavens announce the establishment of the Day of Judgment and the setting up of another system in the universe. Human effort and striving will bear fruit on the Day of Judgment, and the record of deeds of the righteous and the wicked will be given to them so that they may receive reward or punishment based on it. This is the tradition (Sunnah) of the Lord throughout history, and everyone will be judged based on it. It is mentioned in narrations that reciting the verses of this Sūrah with contemplation protects a person and keeps him safe from having his record of deeds given to his left hand. (18)

18) Tafsir Nūr ath-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ إِنَّكَ كَادِحٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَدْحًا فَمُلَـٰقِيهِ (٦)
O man! You are labouring toward your Lord laboriously, and you will encounter Him. (6)
فَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَـٰبَهُۥ بِيَمِينِهِۦ (۷)
Then, as for him who is given his record [of deeds] in his right hand, (7)
فَسَوْفَ يُحَاسَبُ حِسَابًا يَسِيرًا (۸)
he will receive an easy reckoning, (8)
وَيَنقَلِبُ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِۦ مَسْرُورًا (۹)
and he will return to his folks joyfully. (9)
وَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَـٰبَهُۥ وَرَآءَ ظَهْرِهِۦ (۱٠)
But as for him who is given his record from behind his back, (10)
فَسَوْفَ يَدْعُوا۟ ثُبُورًا (۱۱)
he will pray for annihilation (11)
وَيَصْلَىٰ سَعِيرًا (۱۲)
and enter the Blaze. (12)
إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ فِىٓ أَهْلِهِۦ مَسْرُورًا (۱۳)
Indeed he used to be joyful among his folk, (13)
إِنَّهُۥ ظَنَّ أَن لَّن يَحُورَ (۱٤)
and he thought that he would never return. (14)
بَلَىٰٓ إِنَّ رَبَّهُۥ كَانَ بِهِۦ بَصِيرًا (۱٥)
Yes, his Lord had been watching him. (15)

Key Points

The word kadḥ (كَدح) means both effort and striving, and also causing harm or injury. This means that man proceeds toward his Lord, but it is a journey accompanied by pain and hardship, as if he is inflicting harm and injury upon himself. We read in a hadith: Whoever possesses three characteristics, Allah will make his reckoning easy and admit him into Paradise by His mercy: He gives to the one who deprived him; He maintains ties of kinship with the one who cut him off; and He forgives the one who wronged him. «تعطى من حرمك تصل من قطعك و تعفو عمن ظلمك»(21)

In Ayahs 9 and 13, the discussion is about joy and happiness among the family. One is negative, which is criticized (Ayah 9), and one is positive, which is the reward for the righteous (Ayah 13). Indeed, the fruit of drunken and heedless joy in this world is Hellfire, and the fruit of faith and piety in this world is joy among relatives on the Day of Judgment. Imam Ali عليه السلام was asked about the Ayah «ینقلب الى اهله مسرورا» (He will return to his family rejoicing). He said: Those who were the family and dependents of the person destined for Paradise in this world, if they are believers, they will also be his family in the Hereafter.

21) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Man is moving toward Allah. «یا ایّها الانسان انّك كادح الى ربّك» (Just as He says elsewhere: «انّا للّه و انّا الیه راجعون»(22))

2- Man faces countless difficulties on the path of movement toward Allah. «انّك كادح الى ربّك»

3- Allah has established fixed laws in history and society that everyone follows; they can neither stop, nor turn back, nor go in another direction. «كادح الى ربّك كدحا»

4- The end of man's movement is reaching Allah. «فملاقیه»

5- Man's deeds in this world are recorded and will be presented to him on the Day of Judgment in a documented and written form. «اوتى كتابه»

6- On the Day of Judgment, the reckoning of people is varied, and the righteous will have an easy reckoning. «حساباً یسیراً» (While the wicked will have a severe reckoning. «حساباً شدیداً»(23))

7- Do not be deceived by worldly joys, for they pass; think about the lasting joys of the Day of Judgment. (If today, for the sake of the Divine command, we forgo some family pleasures and distance ourselves from family for Jihad, seeking knowledge, or service, it will be compensated in the future.) «ینقلب الى اهله مسرورا»

8- On the Day when some flee from one another: «یوم یفرّ المرء من اخیه...»(24), the one whose reckoning is easy and whose abode is Paradise will return to his family. «ینقلب الى اهله مسروراً» (Perhaps those fleeing from one another are the disbelievers.)

22) Al-Baqarah, 156.

23) Al-Talaq, 8.

24) Abasa, 34 - 36.

Ayahs

فَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِٱلشَّفَقِ (۱٦)
I swear by the evening glow, (16)
وَٱلَّيْلِ وَمَا وَسَقَ (۱۷)
by the night and what it is fraught with, (17)
وَٱلْقَمَرِ إِذَا ٱتَّسَقَ (۱۸)
by the moon when it blooms full: (18)
لَتَرْكَبُنَّ طَبَقًا عَن طَبَقٍ (۱۹)
you will surely fare from stage to stage. (19)
فَمَا لَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۲٠)
What is the matter with them that they will not believe, (20)
وَإِذَا قُرِئَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ لَا يَسْجُدُونَ ۩ (۲۱)
and will not prostrate when the Quran is recited to them? (21)

Key Points

Yahuur (یَحور) is derived from Hoor (حور), meaning return or reversion. What is meant by it is the return to the life of the Hereafter on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). Tabaq (طَبَق) means conformity and correspondence, meaning the placement of two things upon one another. It is also used to refer to a state or condition, because the human soul conforms to that state. Thus, latarkabunna tabaqan 'an tabaq (لتركبن طبقاً عن طبق) means that you will mount a state or characteristic which, like a conveyance, carries you toward your destination. (25) Just as redness appears at the threshold of sunset, then the darkness of night covers it, and then the light of the moon shines upon the darkness of the night—and these states come one after another—you humans also come one after another and possess various states on the path of perfection toward Allah. What is meant by latarkabunna tabaqan 'an tabaq (لتركبنّ طبقاً عن طبق) are the different stages of life that man traverses on His journey toward his Lord: the worldly life, the life of the Barzakh (intermediate realm), and the life of the Hereafter. (26) It is possible that the meaning of latarkabunna tabaqan 'an tabaq (لتركبنّ طبقاً عن طبق) is that your destiny follows the destiny of past nations. If you deny like them, you will be destroyed, and if you believe like them, you will be saved. A tradition (Hadith) also confirms this meaning. What is meant by prostration (sujud) in the Ayah, «اذا قرى ء القرآن لا یسجدون» (When the Qur'an is recited, they do not prostrate), is humility, affirmation, and faith; otherwise, people would have to fall into prostration upon the recitation of every single Ayah.

25) Tafsir Rahnamā. 26) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The source of human misery is heedlessness of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) or its denial. «ظنَّ ان لن یحور»

2- Deniers of the Resurrection deny based on conjecture, not knowledge. «ظنّ ان لن یحور»

3- The root of drunken pleasures is heedlessness of the Hereafter. «كان فى اهله مسرورا... ظنّ ان لن یحور»

4- Reason and caution dictate that if you do not have certainty about the Resurrection, you should at least have a strong presumption of its occurrence, «یظنّون انهم ملاقوا ربّهم» (27), and not presume its non-occurrence, which is the source of destruction. «ظنّ ان لن یحور»

5- The result of reciting the Qur'an must be servitude and submission. «اذا قرى ء... القرآن لا یسجدون» 27) Al-Baqarah, 46.

Ayahs

بَلِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يُكَذِّبُونَ (۲۲)
Indeed, the faithless impugn [the Apostle], (22)
وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يُوعُونَ (۲۳)
and Allah knows best what they keep to themselves. (23)
فَبَشِّرْهُم بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۲٤)
So inform them of a painful punishment, (24)
إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍۭ (۲٥)
excepting such as are faithful and do righteous deeds: for them there will be an everlasting reward. (25)

Key Points

Yū‘ūn (یوعون) is derived from wi‘ā’ (وِعاء), meaning "container" or "vessel." What is meant by it here is the vessel of the heart and what a person holds within his heart. Bashshir (بَشّر) is derived from bishārah (بشارت), referring to news by which a change occurs in a person's face (basharah), and it is usually applied to good news. Sometimes it is also used for contempt or sarcasm, as when they say: "So-and-so's good news is death."

Messages

1- Disbelievers not only refuse to submit to the verses of the Quran, but they also deny them. «لایسجدون بل... یكذّبون»

2- Whoever accepts the path of disbelief denies every issue they encounter. «بل الّذین كفروا یكذّبون»

3- Allah encompasses the inner secrets of man. «واللّه اعلم بما یوعون»

4- Sometimes, under the guise of denial and rejection, a person pursues other goals, of which Allah is aware. «یكذّبون واللّه اعلم بما یوعون»

5- Sometimes, in order to protect his interests, a person denies something outwardly but approves of it in his heart. «یكذّبون واللّه اعلم بما یوعون»

6- Although the basis of punishment and reward is action, the value of the action lies in its goal and motive, of which Allah is aware. «واللّه اعلم بما یوعون»

7- The prerequisite of faith is the performance of every action that is righteous and worthy, regardless of the field. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات» (Worship is one instance of righteous action.)

8- Faith is inseparable from action; they are mutually necessary. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

9- Divine rewards are never interrupted and are not accompanied by obligation (or reproach). «لهم اجر غیر ممنون»
